item.title: Isa Genzken Finds Chaos in Order
item.description: Also: Raye’s ambitious new album, Nathan Lane’s Willy Loman, Dance Theatre of Harlem’s seminal “Firebird,” and more.
item.dc:creator: ByHilton Als,Emily Nussbaum,Sheldon Pearce,Brian Seibert,andRachel Syme

item.title: A Malaysian Menu Laced with the Flavors of Brooklyn
item.description: Kelang, in Greenpoint, offers a modern, wide-ranging definition of culinary authenticity.
item.dc:creator: ByHelen Rosner

item.title: Trump’s Strategic and Moral Failure in Iran
item.description: From the first day of his Presidency, Trump has posed an emergency to both his country and the world.
item.dc:creator: ByDavid Remnick

item.title: Sam Wang, Politician-in-Training
item.description: The neuroscientist went from analyzing elections to running for Congress. But can this rookie win over New Jersey locals—and Trump voters?
item.dc:creator: ByGabriel Debenedetti

item.title: What Wallace Shawn Did Before His “Moth Days”
item.description: When the two lead actresses in Shawn’s play called in sick, their understudies scrambled to prep in the dressing room. The stand-ins? Deborah Eisenberg and Shawn himself.
item.dc:creator: BySarah Larson

item.title: Sandy Liang Puts a Bow on It
item.description: The designer will add frills to anything—from Dr. Dre headphones to Gap hoodies. At the Frick’s “Ruffles & Ribbons” exhibit, she confronts the deeper meaning of decadence.
item.dc:creator: ByEmilia Petrarca

item.title: Ed Solomon’s Family Portrait
item.description: The screenwriter’s latest film, “The Christophers,” stars Ian McKellen as a lapsed artist. While gallery-hopping, Solomon reflects on his relationship with his painter mother, who recently put down her own paintbrush.
item.dc:creator: ByEmma Allen

item.title: Why I Wanted to Keep My Marriage a Secret
item.description: It’s not that I was embarrassed by Hugh or that I thought someone better might come along. I just shudder when I hear a man say the words “my husband.”
item.dc:creator: ByDavid Sedaris

item.title: Radu Jude, the Bard of Bucharest
item.description: The director’s native city drives him crazy—and drives him to make loony, brilliant films.
item.dc:creator: ByRebecca Mead

item.title: The Car-Crash Conspiracy
item.description: High-speed accidents, crooked lawyers, and poor people desperate for cash—it was the kind of scheme that could have been cooked up only in the Big Easy.
item.dc:creator: ByPatrick Radden Keefe

item.title: The Return of Family Detention
item.description: Under the Trump Administration, thousands of immigrant children have been detained, and many have suffered from medical neglect.
item.dc:creator: BySarah Stillman

item.title: When It Gets Warm . . .
item.description: I will wear the perfect amount of sunscreen so that I don’t look like clown-faced Mark Zuckerberg on that surfboard or red-faced Mark Zuckerberg at a Senate hearing.
item.dc:creator: ByZach ZimmermanandBlythe Roberson

item.title: “A Private View”
item.description: “Oh, not another story about me,” she cried. “Another book about how I was the world’s worst mother. I wish you could find something else to write about.”
item.dc:creator: ByDouglas Stuart

item.title: A Lesson of Vietnam: Getting in Is Easier than Getting Out
item.description: The war was sustained by a seductive delusion: that an unwinnable conflict might still be managed into an outcome short of humiliation.
item.dc:creator: ByLouis Menand

item.title: The Violence in Vermeer
item.description: It is easy to treat the Dutch artist as an agreeable intimist—a transcriber of domestic niceties. But he grew up in a world of war, starvation, and massacres. His paintings were safe havens.
item.dc:creator: ByAnthony Lane

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: “Safe Passage,” “Cave Mountain,” “See You on the Other Side,” and “Almost Life.”

item.title: St. Paul Remade Human History. How Did He Do It?
item.description: New scholarship reconsiders the apostle who turned a Jewish sect into a world religion—and whose legacy remains contested two millennia later.
item.dc:creator: ByAdam Gopnik

item.title: Zara Larsson Gets Her Flowers
item.description: On the “Midnight Sun” tour, the Swedish artist makes a comeback that feels like a début.
item.dc:creator: ByDoreen St. Félix

item.title: “Clarion”
item.description: “There are people who don’t hear an internal monologue or private dialogue in their heads.”
item.dc:creator: ByRae Armantrout

item.title: “Apocalypso”
item.description: “I couldn’t finish the article about / short attention spans either, / armed feds in the Wendy’s, / Saturn slowly losing its rings.”
item.dc:creator: ByDobby Gibson

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the April 20, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Tuesday, April 7, 2026
item.description: A moderately challenging puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByPaolo Pasco
